Title: Melatonin Promotes SGT1-Involved Signals to Ameliorate Drought Stress Adaption in Rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsSGT1 is a conserved SGT1 cochaperone protein previously implicated in disease resistance and abiotic stresses. In this study, CRISPR edits targeted the first exon of OsSGT1. Mutation of OsSGT1 specifically disrupts melatonin-induced increases in SA and JA and the associated signaling gene expression, while ABA accumulation under drought still occurs, supporting an ABA-independent role of OsSGT1 in melatonin signaling.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsSGT1 expression is induced by drought stress and further enhanced by melatonin in rice seedlings.
2. CRISPR knockout of OsSGT1 (sgt1 mutants) abolishes melatonin-mediated improvements in growth, survival, water status, stomatal conductance, photosynthesis, and osmotic adjustment under severe drought.
3. OsSGT1 is required for melatonin-induced suppression of ROS and oxidative damage and for restoration of antioxidant enzyme activities and ASA/GSH levels under drought.
4. OsSGT1 mutation prevents melatonin-dependent restoration of SA and JA contents and associated signaling gene expression and strongly reduces OsABI5 expression under drought.
5. Double mutants of OsSGT1 and OsABI5 completely lose melatonin-induced activation of antioxidant genes (OsSODA1, OsCAT2, OsAPX4, OsGR2), supporting a model in which melatonin promotes SGT1-involved signals to activate ABI5 and antioxidant defenses against drought.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsSGT1 abolishes the beneficial effects of melatonin on drought-stressed rice seedlings, leading to reduced growth and survival, impaired stomatal conductance and water status, sustained osmolyte accumulation, elevated ROS and oxidative damage, weakened antioxidant enzyme activities, and failure to restore ABA, SA, and JA levels under drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsSGT1 acts upstream of OsABI5 to positively regulate its expression and the expression of antioxidant-responsive genes under drought, mediating melatonin-induced activation of the antioxidant system in an ABA-independent but SA/JA-associated manner.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Melatonin–SGT1–ABI5–antioxidant signaling pathway under drought, involving crosstalk with ABA (ABA accumulation and ABI5 induction), and SA/JA pathways that modulate SGT1 expression and downstream antioxidant responses.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsSGT1 --(activation)--> OsABI5; OsSGT1/OsABI5 --(activation)--> OsSODA1, OsCAT2, OsAPX4, OsGR2, promoting antioxidant defenses under drought with melatonin.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsSGT1 transcript levels in leaves are induced by drought stress and further increased by melatonin treatment; OsSGT1 expression is higher under drought than under control in WT, abi5, and sgt1-1abi5 backgrounds, whereas exogenous ABA feeding under non-stress does not significantly alter OsSGT1 expression.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Severe drought stress imposed as 25% field capacity; foliar spraying with 100 µM melatonin every two days during drought; exogenous 10 µM ABA treatments under 100% or 25% field capacity.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought), melatonin signaling, antioxidant system regulation
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought stress tolerance, oxidative stress response)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought stress, survival rates decreased by 46.12% in WT and 59.3% in sgt1; melatonin significantly improved survival, RWC, chlorophyll content, and photosynthetic rate in WT but caused no significant changes in sgt1; drought-induced ABA increased 4.79-fold in WT and 3.29–3.94-fold in sgt1, while SA and JA were decreased and could be restored by melatonin only in WT.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: OsSGT1 mutation in rice seedlings under drought leads to sustained high soluble sugar and proline levels despite melatonin treatment, indicating persistent osmotic stress, and prevents melatonin-induced restoration of SA and JA contents and expression of OsMYC2 and OsbHLH6.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa L. japonica wild-type seedlings; CRISPR/Cas9-derived sgt1-1 and sgt1-2 mutants with edits in the first exon of OsSGT1; abi5-1 and abi5-2 mutants; double mutants sgt1-1abi5-1 and sgt1-1abi5-2; seedlings grown in 1/2 MS liquid medium then transferred to soil for drought and hormone treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing and Agrobacterium-mediated transformation; drought treatments at defined field capacities; foliar melatonin spraying at multiple concentrations; ABA feeding in solution culture; measurements of survival rate, plant height, fresh weight, relative water content, chlorophyll content, photosynthetic rate, stomatal morphology and conductance, soluble sugar and proline; assays of H2O2, MDA, and O2-; antioxidant enzyme (SOD, CAT, APX, GR) activities and ASA/GSH contents; LC-MS quantification of ABA, SA, JA; qRT-PCR analysis of OsSGT1, OsABI5, OsMYC2, OsbHLH6, OsSODA1, OsCAT2, OsAPX4, and OsGR2.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of OsSGT1 (sgt1-1, sgt1-2) targeting the first exon, generation of OsSGT1–OsABI5 double mutants, followed by drought and melatonin treatments and assessment of growth, survival, stomatal behavior, osmolytes, ROS, antioxidant enzyme activities, phytohormones, and expression of OsABI5 and antioxidant genes by qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: OsSGT1 was identified as the rice homolog of SGT1 via homology search in the Rice Annotation Project Database (RAP-DB) and functionally characterized using CRISPR/Cas9-generated knockout mutants and genetic crosses with abi5 mutants.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsSGT1 is a key positive regulator required for melatonin-mediated drought tolerance in rice. Enhancing OsSGT1 function, together with foliar melatonin application, improves seedling survival, photosynthesis, antioxidant capacity, and hormone balance (ABA, SA, JA) under severe drought, making OsSGT1 a promising target for developing drought-resilient rice cultivars and for melatonin-based agronomic management.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsABI5 is an ABA signaling component whose mutation causes ABA-insensitive phenotypes. In this study, abi5 and sgt1-1abi5 mutants showed no change in ABA content after melatonin treatment and did not respond to exogenous ABA at the level of antioxidant activation, supporting that OsABI5 functions downstream of ABA and OsSGT1 in mediating melatonin-enhanced drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsABI5 transcripts are strongly induced by drought and further upregulated by melatonin and ABA in WT rice seedlings.
2. In sgt1 mutants and double mutants, OsABI5 expression is not properly induced by drought, melatonin, or ABA, indicating that OsABI5 acts downstream of OsSGT1 in this context.
3. abi5 and sgt1-1abi5 mutants show reduced growth and survival and elevated ROS accumulation under drought, and melatonin fails to confer protection, demonstrating that OsABI5 is required for melatonin-mediated drought tolerance.
4. Mutation of OsABI5 (alone or with OsSGT1) abolishes melatonin-induced activation of antioxidant genes (OsSODA1, OsCAT2, OsAPX4, OsGR2) and the associated increases in antioxidant enzyme activities.
5. The data support a model in which melatonin promotes OsSGT1-dependent induction of OsABI5, which in turn activates antioxidant defenses to restore redox homeostasis under drought.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsABI5 is required for melatonin-mediated alleviation of drought stress in rice; abi5 and sgt1-1abi5 mutants exhibit weak growth, low survival, high ROS accumulation, and impaired antioxidant systems under drought, and they fail to benefit from melatonin or respond normally to ABA accumulation and feeding.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsABI5 acts downstream of OsSGT1 and ABA to activate antioxidant genes under drought; melatonin promotes OsABI5 expression largely through OsSGT1 in an ABA-independent manner, thereby enhancing antioxidant capacity and ROS scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA–ABI5 signaling pathway and melatonin–SGT1–ABI5–antioxidant pathway under drought, integrating ABA accumulation with melatonin-induced modulation of ABA, SA, and JA to regulate antioxidant gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsSGT1 --(activation)--> OsABI5; OsABI5 --(activation)--> OsSODA1, OsCAT2, OsAPX4, OsGR2, enhancing antioxidant defenses under drought with melatonin and ABA.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In WT, OsABI5 expression in leaves is upregulated by drought stress and further induced by melatonin under drought; exogenous ABA feeding also strongly induces OsABI5 expression. In sgt1 mutants and sgt1-1abi5 double mutants, OsABI5 expression is markedly reduced or shows no induction under drought, melatonin, or ABA treatments.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Severe drought stress (25% field capacity), foliar 100 µM melatonin treatment, and 10 µM ABA feeding under well-watered and drought conditions.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: ABA and melatonin signaling in drought stress tolerance and regulation of antioxidant gene expression
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought stress tolerance, ABA responsiveness, oxidative stress response)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought, ABA contents in abi5 and sgt1-1abi5 mutants increased significantly relative to control, but melatonin treatment did not significantly alter ABA levels; oxidant products (MDA and H2O2) increased approximately 2.5–2.8-fold and O2- production was greatly induced, while antioxidant enzyme activities and GSH/ASA contents were significantly decreased and not restored by melatonin.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: abi5 and sgt1-1abi5 mutants under drought show strongly increased MDA, H2O2, and O2- production and markedly decreased activities of SOD, CAT, APX, GR and contents of GSH and ASA, even after melatonin treatment, indicating loss of antioxidant system responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa L. japonica wild-type seedlings; abi5-1 and abi5-2 mutants; double mutants sgt1-1abi5-1 and sgt1-1abi5-2 obtained by crossing sgt1-1 with abi5-1 or abi5-2; seedlings grown in 1/2 MS medium then transferred to soil for drought and hormone treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genetic analysis of abi5 single and sgt1-1abi5 double mutants; drought and melatonin treatments; ABA feeding; phenotyping of seedling growth and survival; measurement of ABA content, ROS (H2O2, MDA, O2-), antioxidant enzyme activities and ASA/GSH; and qRT-PCR analysis of OsABI5, OsSGT1, and antioxidant and hormone-signaling genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of abi5-1 and abi5-2 loss-of-function mutants (previously generated) and OsSGT1–OsABI5 double mutants, combined with drought and melatonin treatments, ABA feeding, and measurements of growth, survival, ABA content, ROS, antioxidant enzyme activities, and expression of OsABI5 and antioxidant genes by qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: OsABI5 was identified via homology search in the Rice Annotation Project Database and previously characterized; here its function was further dissected using existing abi5 mutants and new OsSGT1–OsABI5 double mutants in drought and melatonin response assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsABI5 is a central positive regulator of drought tolerance downstream of ABA and OsSGT1, required for melatonin-induced activation of antioxidant defenses. Manipulating OsABI5 activity, together with exogenous melatonin application, could improve drought tolerance, although its classical role in ABA-mediated growth restraint suggests that fine-tuning its expression or activity will be critical for breeding drought-resilient yet productive rice cultivars.
